trivia_bullets.text: Spawnbinder Mage is a Human Wizard Ally — a mage in the Zendikari resistance who has learned to trap and restrain Eldrazi Spawn. The 'Spawnbinder' name positions this figure as a specialized anti-Eldrazi operative, consistent with the OGW narrative that allied forces adapted their tactics specifically to the Eldrazi threat. [Scryfall type_line]

Flavor text: "Follow me. Step lightly. And resist the urge to kick them." [Scryfall] The humor is sharp — a tactical command not to provoke captured Eldrazi spawn in transit. The throwaway 'urge to kick them' line implies the spawn are conscious enough to react, which reinforces the metabolic-indifference framing: they're dangerous but not actively malicious, more like caged animals than enemy soldiers.

Cohort — tap this creature and an untapped Ally to tap target creature — was OGW's keyword for Ally cooperation. The Cohort trigger requires the tapped Ally to have been under your control since your last turn began, which prevents instant-speed abuse with newly cast creatures. Spawnbinder Mage is Pauper-legal. Only one printing exists. EDHREC rank 27010. [Scryfall; Wizards OGW Mechanics article]
